URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9840515/

SUMMARY:

enriched_points.expanded_content: Genetic factors substantially impact psychiatric disorders, sharing influences with behavioral traits, cognitive functions, and various somatic diseases, emphasizing their clinical relevance.
Psychiatric conditions exhibit intricate genetic architectures, complicating their diagnosis and treatment due to overlapping symptoms and shared genetic underpinnings.
A network-based perspective is essential for understanding the interactions among genetic, environmental, and behavioral factors, fostering comprehensive insights into mental health.
